---
title: GEO + SRA (NCBI)
alias: &ALIAS geo-sra
href: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

NCBI's Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) and Sequence Read Archive (SRA) together provide the world's largest publicly accessible repositories for high-throughput functional genomics and raw sequencing data.

## Key Features

- GEO hosts processed functional genomics datasets including microarray, RNA-seq, ChIP-seq, and ATAC-seq
- SRA archives raw sequencing reads from all next-generation sequencing platforms and assay types
- Linked data model connects GEO datasets to raw SRA runs and NCBI BioProject/BioSample records
- SRA Toolkit enables efficient programmatic download and streaming of raw sequencing data
- Supports submission of metagenomics, single-cell, and spatial transcriptomic datasets

## Use Cases in Research

- Downloading raw metagenomic sequencing reads for microbiome composition re-analysis
- Accessing transcriptomic datasets from pathogen-infected cell lines and animal models
- Mining GEO for AMR-related gene expression signatures across experimental conditions
- Building large training corpora for genomic AI/ML models from publicly available SRA data
- Conducting meta-analyses of host-pathogen transcriptional interactions across multiple studies

## Getting Started

Visit [GEO + SRA (NCBI)](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
